text.title: AI-Enhanced Design Systems Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-enhanced-design-systems methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: AI MUST be applied only on top of an already mature DS (tokens codified, naming consistent, stories ≥80 %).
rule.rationale: AI on a chaotic system locks in the chaos.

rule.statement: Documentation MUST be generated from the component source + stories, not from a free-text prompt.
rule.rationale: Free-text docs diverge from code within one release.

rule.statement: Token-violation scanner MUST block merge on hardcoded values.
rule.rationale: Non-blocking warnings are ignored at scale.

rule.statement: Generated variant permutations MUST pass visual-regression before merge.
rule.rationale: AI-generated variants frequently break layout in subtle ways.

rule.statement: Generated documentation MUST be human-reviewed before publish.
rule.rationale: AI docs hallucinate prop semantics + accessibility claims.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
